event: edema of the right gluteal region
CT of the abdomen and the pelvis
extensive inflammatory infiltration of the subcutaneous tissues of the hypogastrium and the penis
liquefaction and presence of gas in the subcutaneous tissues of the scrotum, the perineum, and the right gluteal region
urgery in the Department of Urology
necrosis of the tissues within the right gluteal area
area after wide debridement of the necrotic tissues of perineum, scrotum, hypogastrium, and right gluteal region
transferred to the intensive care unit
able to breathe on his own with oxygen on low flow
diuresis was stimulated using a small dose of furosemide
postoperatively, the inflammatory markers decreased significantly
culture of the pus material showed Escherichia coli and Pseudomonas aeruginosa
antibiotic therapy was modified to include cephazolin
transferred to the Department of Plastic Surgery
free-skin grafts could be applied to the debrided areas
currently, the patient is living in a nursing home
receives testosterone supplementation at a dose of 80 mg twice daily
will be qualified for colostomy reversal resume the continuity and proper functioning of the gastrointestinal tract
condition after applying the free-skin grafts in locations after debridement of the necrotic tissues
